symptoms.symptom_name: Acute high-frequency watery, non-bloody diarrhoea (~10 motions in 5 hours)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Watery non-bloody diarrhoea is the hallmark symptom of a microscopic colitis flare.
Sudden high-frequency watery diarrhoea is a common infective presentation.
High-volume ongoing losses are the mechanism of volume depletion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Crampy lower abdominal pain before each motion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-defecation cramping is common in colitic flares but non-specific.
Mild diffuse cramping without pain out of proportion neither confirms nor excludes ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural dizziness and profound weakness ('can barely stand')

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptomatic postural change after high diarrhoeal losses is a clinical marker of volume depletion until proven otherwise.

symptoms.symptom_name: No blood and no black/tarry stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ischaemic colitis characteristically produces bloody diarrhoea; its absence lowers the probability.
No visible blood or melaena reduces suspicion of active bleeding (caveat: bismuth can mask melaena).
Absence of fever lowers but does not exclude C. difficile or invasive bacterial colitis.
Afebrile status reduces the likelihood of systemic infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No recent antibiotics and no recent travel

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No recent antibiotic exposure lowers the pre-test probability of C. difficile.
No travel history reduces exposure-related infective causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known biopsy-proven chronic watery-diarrhoea bowel condition

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pre-existing biopsy diagnosis makes a flare of the same condition the most parsimonious cause — but is also the anchoring bait.

symptoms.symptom_name: Over-the-counter loperamide taken today with minimal effect

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Poor response to antimotility agents is non-specific and does not discriminate between causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: No orthostatic BP drop on examination (sitting 118/75, standing 115/78), capillary refill 2s, moist mucous membranes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician measurement: the euvolaemic exam is what safely excluded significant dehydration in person — the AI Doctor cannot obtain it and must not assume it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-peritonitic abdomen with hyperactive bowel sounds; no guarding, rigidity or rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician examination excluding peritonism — unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs on clinician measurement (BP 118/75, HR 92, afebrile 36.8, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician vitals showing haemodynamic stability — the AI cannot measure these and infers circulatory status only from reported symptoms.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stool C. difficile toxin/PCR and culture

investigation_details.investigation_name: Stool C. difficile toxin/PCR and bacterial culture
investigation_details.expected_result: Not performed in this encounter; would be needed to exclude C. difficile / invasive bacterial colitis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: C. difficile or invasive bacterial colitis remains unexcluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Toxin/PCR testing is the definitive way to confirm or exclude C. difficile.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum electrolytes, urea/creatinine and full blood count

investigation_details.expected_result: Not performed in this encounter; would quantify dehydration, electrolyte loss and any acute kidney injury
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of dehydration, hypokalaemia and AKI remains unquantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bloods objectively quantify volume/electrolyte depletion and renal impact.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Volume-depletion signal in an isolated older adult
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: High-frequency watery losses plus symptomatic postural dizziness in someone alone overnight is the safety-critical combination that drives the telehealth escalation, independent of the underlying colitic cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person orthostatic (lying-to-standing) blood pressure and hydration assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The decisive hydration data cannot be obtained via chat; a same-day in-person check of orthostatic vitals, capillary refill and mucous membranes is the core action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Stool C. difficile toxin/PCR and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Acute colitis in an older adult warrants a C. difficile and infective screen even without a clear antibiotic history.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Electrolytes, renal function and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies dehydration, electrolyte loss and acute kidney injury to guide rehydration route (oral versus IV).
The diagnosis is already biopsy-established; endoscopy has no role in managing an acute flare and should not be ordered from this encounter.
Not indicated without features of an acute surgical abdomen or ischaemia (no bleeding, no peritonism, pain not out of proportion); avoid over-investigation.
